questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who is the Supreme Commander of the Indian Armed Forces?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The President of India is the Supreme Commander of the Indian Armed Forces.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.subTopic: Elections and Representation of People Act
questions.question: Which body is entrusted with recruiting India's civil servants on the basis of merit?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The Union Public Service Commission recruits civil servants on the basis of merit.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which renewable energy source is dependent on the gravitational pull of the moon?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Tidal energy depends on the gravitational pull of the moon.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Indian Light Tank (ILT) has achieved a major feat in December 2024 by firing multiple rounds at various ranges at an altitude of over 4200 m?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The Zorawar light tank achieved this feat in December 2024.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which sectors were emphasised in the Eighth Five-Year Plan for employment generation?
options: Animal husbandry, fishery, horticulture and rural industries
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The Eighth Five-Year Plan emphasised animal husbandry, fishery, horticulture and rural industries for employment generation.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In 2025, the African Union (AU) admitted which country as its newest member?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Tanzania was admitted as the newest member of the African Union in 2025.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In 2025, which city in France hosted the French Open tennis tournament?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The French Open is hosted in Paris, France.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which novel macrolide antibiotic, highlighted in a December 2024 PIB factsheet, is being positioned as India's first indigenous drug against community-acquired pneumonia?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Naffthromycin is being positioned as India's first indigenous drug against community-acquired pneumonia.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who were the Voharikas in the judicial structure of the Magadha period?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Voharikas were Lawyer-Judges in the Magadha period.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Cabinet minister Kinjarapu Ram Mohan Naidu was related to which ministry in May 2025, who was named in the World Economic Forum (WEF) Young Global Leaders list 2025?
options: Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Kinjarapu Ram Mohan Naidu was related to the Ministry of Civil Aviation.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: To change the desktop wallpaper, which option would you choose?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Desktop wallpaper is changed through Settings > Personalization.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: When did the Hershey-Chase experiment provide solid evidence that DNA, not proteins, is the genetic material?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The Hershey-Chase experiment was conducted in 1952.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which edition of the Indian National Congress session was held in Haridwar in 1921?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The 58th session of the Indian National Congress was held in Haridwar in 1921.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following shooting events (in distance) did Simranpreet Kaur Brar win silver medal in the women's category at ISSF World Cup 2025, Lima?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Simranpreet Kaur Brar won silver in the 25m event.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Article of the Indian Constitution directs the State to promote the welfare of the people?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Article 38 directs the State to promote the welfare of the people.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What is the main function of RAM in a computer?
options: To provide temporary storage for running programs
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. RAM provides temporary storage for running programs.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which indigenously developed missile was successfully test fired by DRDO and Indian Navy on 26 March 2025?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The Vertically Launched Short-Range Surface-to-Air Missile was test fired on 26 March 2025.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which chronic disease do the inner walls of the lungs' air sacs, called alveoli, become damaged, causing them to eventually burst?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Emphysema damages the alveoli in the lungs.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who is known as the 'Grand Old Man of India' and promoted Indian economic nationalism?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Dadabhai Naoroji is known as the 'Grand Old Man of India'.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which range is known for its rich biodiversity and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The Western Ghats are a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for rich biodiversity.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following is the female reproductive part of a flower?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Gynoecium is the female reproductive part of a flower.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: serves as the conventional base material for the art form known as Kalamkari.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Cloth serves as the conventional base material for Kalamkari art.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following years was the Bengal Sati Regulation enacted?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The Bengal Sati Regulation was enacted in 1829.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the Planning Commission of India?
options: A constitutional body established by an Article of the Constitution.
A statutory body created by an Act of Parliament in 1950.
A constitutional commission chaired by the Chief Justice of India.
An extra-constitutional advisory body created by a government resolution in 1950.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The Planning Commission was an extra-constitutional advisory body created by a government resolution in 1950.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: [October 2024] Mongla Port, recently seen in the news for strategic development and trade ties, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Mongla Port is located in Bangladesh.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which Indian institution has developed an affordable device that is capable of rapidly separating plasma from blood and purifying water, priced at ₹1,000?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. IIIT-Allahabad developed this affordable device.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In 2025, which of the following institutes developed the paper-based technology 'Freshness Keeper' to extend the freshness of cut flowers?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Indian Institute of Horticulture Research developed 'Freshness Keeper'.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which of the following rivers is associated with National Waterway 10 (NW-10)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The Amba river is associated with National Waterway 10.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: A free trade arrangement of the SAARC countries, the South Asian Free Trade Area (SAFTA), came into force in which year?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. SAFTA came into force in 2006.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: भारत में फ़कीर-सन्यासी विद्रोह के साथ देबी चौधुरानी और भवानी पाठक का संबंध निम्नलिखित में से क्या इंगित करता है?
options: ये विद्रोह विशुद्ध रूप से धार्मिक प्रकृति के थे।
इस विद्रोह में सभी धर्मों और लिंगों की भागीदारी थी।
बंगाल में केवल जनजाति समूहों ने ही ऐसे प्रतिरोधों का नेतृत्व किया।
इस अंदोलन पर उच्च जाति के कुलीन वर्ग का प्रभुत्व था।
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The involvement of Devi Chaudhurani and Bhavani Pathak indicates participation of all religions and genders in the revolt.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Barapani Lake of Shillong is also known as ________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Barapani Lake is also known as Umiam Lake.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Who is the head of the civil administration of a district court in India?
options: Registrar General of the High Court
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The District Judge is the head of the civil administration of a district court.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In which of the following years was the National Seeds Policy launched?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The National Seeds Policy was launched in 2002.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Decathlon, which set up its first outlet in India in Bengaluru in 2009, is a ________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Decathlon is a French sports goods retailer.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Volcanoes are often found in which type of plate boundary?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Volcanoes are often found at convergent and divergent boundaries.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: What was the official theme of India's 76th Republic Day Parade held on January 26, 2025?
options: Har Ghar Tiranga: Unity in Diversity
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The theme was 'Viksit Bharat: Innovation & Inclusion'.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: The sculptures on the Sanchi Stupa primarily depict which of the following?
options: Mythical creatures like yakshas and kinnaras
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The Sanchi Stupa sculptures primarily depict Jataka story narratives.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: In 2025, which two countries were involved in renewed tensions over the South China Sea, prompting international concern about maritime security?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. China and the Philippines were involved in renewed tensions over the South China Sea.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: सातवाहन साम्राज्य के दौरान, 'आहार' या 'राष्ट्र' शब्द किसको संदर्भित करते थे?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. 'Aahara' or 'Rashtra' referred to a district during the Satavahana empire.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.question: Which government initiative primarily focuses on improving human development by ensuring healthcare and nutrition for women and children?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Poshan Abhiyaan focuses on healthcare and nutrition for women and children.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The angles of elevation of the top of a tower from two points X and Y (on opposite sides of the tower) at distances b2 m and a2 m, respectively, from the base and in the same straight line with it are complementary. The height (in m) of the tower is _____.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The height of the tower is ab.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the value of [954 / {30 + 9 x (3 - 6)}].
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The value is 319.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Prashant and Nisha have to travel from Delhi to Kanpur in their respective cars. Prashant is driving at 117 km/hr while Nisha is driving at 48 km/hr. Find the time taken by Nisha to reach Kanpur if Prashant takes 16 hours.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Nisha takes 39 hours to reach Kanpur.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A sum of money at simple interest amounts to 65,000 in 5 years and to 85,000 in 10 years. Find the principal.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The principal is 44,035.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 82 cm, 18 cm, and 80 cm. What is its area? (in cm^2)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The area of the triangle is 720 cm^2.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: By how much is 65% of 65 greater than 1/5 of 15?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The difference is 39.25.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Jaya can do a certain piece of work in 30 days. Jaya and Sushma can together do the same work in 21 days, and Jaya, Sushma and Hina can do the same work together in 14 days. In how many days can Jaya and Hina do the same work together?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Jaya and Hina can do the work together in 35 days.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: An item marked at ₹5,000 is given at successive discounts of 10% and 5%. Find the final selling price.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The final selling price is 4,275.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: M and N start a business. M invests 46,000 more than N for 3 months and N invests for 4 months. M's share is 453 more than that of N, out of a total profit of 1,359. Find the capital contributed by M.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. M's capital contribution is 73,608.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the mean proportional between 24 and 150.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The mean proportional is 60.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Train A leaves station M at 8:25 AM and reaches station N at 3:25 PM on the same day. Train B leaves station N at 10:25 AM and reaches station M at 3:25 PM on the same day. Find the time when trains A and B meet.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The trains meet at 12:50 PM.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The number 327087 is divisible by which of the following?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. 327087 is divisible by 3.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the greatest number that will divide 49, 97 and 189 so as to leave the same remainder in each case.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The greatest number is 4.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Simplify: 3(((7/3)x^2) - 25x + 12) - 7(x^2 + 9x - 13).
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Simplifying gives -138x + 127.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The selling price of 20 books is equal to the cost price of 5 books. Find the loss or gain percentage.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. There is a loss of 75 percent.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: A can do a piece of work in 20 days while B can do it in 30 days. With the help of C, they finish the work in 10 days. In how many days can C alone do the work?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. C alone can do the work in 4 9/11 days.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: ABCD is a trapezium in which BC || AD and AC = CD. If angle ABC = 19 degrees and angle BAC = 137 degrees, then what is the measure of angle ACD (in degree)?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The measure of angle ACD is 132 degrees.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: If the 6-digit number N83M92 is divisible by 11, then which of the options below can give a possible correct relation between M and N?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. M - N = 1 is the correct relation.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: एक दुकानदार ₹3,500 में एक साइकिल खरीदता है और उसे 20% की छूट पर बेचता है, जिसमें कोई लाभ या हानि नहीं होती। साइकिल का अंकित मूल्य क्या है?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The marked price of the cycle is ₹4,375.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: Find the area of a triangle with base 12 cm and height 8 cm.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The area of the triangle is 48 cm squared.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: 3 वर्ष पहले, पिता की आयु उसके पुत्र की आयु के दोगुने से 24 वर्ष अधिक थी। अब से कितने वर्षों बाद, उसकी आयु उसके पुत्र की आयु की दोगुनी हो जाएगी?

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.question: The average runs scored by a batsman in 23 matches is 42. In the next 10 matches, the batsman scored an average of 15 runs. Find his average runs scored (rounded off to two decimal places) in all the 33 matches.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The average runs scored in all 33 matches is 33.82.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Each of the digits in the number 71642938 is arranged in ascending order from left to right. The position of how many digits will remain unchanged as compared to that in the original number?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. One digit will remain unchanged.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. B sits third to the right of E. F sits to the immediate left of B. C is the immediate neighbour of A and B. G is not the immediate neighbour of F. How many people sit between D and G when counted from the left of G?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. Three people sit between D and G when counted from the left of G.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What will come in place of the question mark (?) in the following equation, if '+' and '÷' are interchanged and '-' and 'x' are interchanged? 38 / 7 + 5 x 20 - 4 = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The value is 22.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, A + B means 'A is the daughter of B', A - B means 'A is the wife of B', A x B means 'A is the father of B' and A ÷ B means 'A is the brother of B'. How is A related to S if 'A + E - F x N ÷ S'?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. A is the father's sister of S.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: If 2 is added to each even digit and 2 is added to each odd digit in the number 7145362, what will be the sum of the last two and first two digits in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The sum of the digits is 24.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Read the given statements and conclusions carefully. Assuming that the information given in the statements is true, even if it appears to be at variance with commonly known facts, decide which of the given conclusion(s) logically follow(s) from the statements. Statements: All physicists are chemists. No chemists are biologists. Conclusions: I. No biologists are physicists. II. Some biologists are physicists.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Only conclusion (I) follows.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: यदि '+' और '÷' को परस्पर बदल दिया जाए, तथा '-' और '×' को परस्पर बदल दिया जाए, तो निम्नलिखित समीकरण में प्रश्न चिह्न (?) के स्थान पर क्या आएगा? 21 ÷ 57 - 3 × 8 + 4 = ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The value is 23.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'MADE' is coded as '4172' and 'HAME' is coded as '2475'. What is the code for 'H' in the given code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The code for 'H' is 5.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 27 35 51 75 107 ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The next number in the series is 147.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In a certain code language, 'ALPS' is coded as '3257' and 'PUNE' is coded as '1346'. What is the code for 'P' in the given code language?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D. The code for 'P' is 3.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Refer to the following number and symbol series and answer the question that follows. Counting to be done from left to right only. (Note: all numbers are single digit numbers only.) (Left) 1 & 96 > 75*7 + 5*70*4% S (Right) How many such symbols are there, each of which is immediately preceded by a number and also immediately followed by a number?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. There are 9 symbols that meet the condition.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Seven boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept above A. Only one box is kept between F and A. Only three boxes are kept between F and E. E is kept at some place above A. B is kept immediately below E. G is kept at one of the places above D. C is not kept immediately above or below F. How many boxes are kept above B?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. Four boxes are kept above B.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? LDH 14, NFJ 17, PHL 20, RJN 23, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. TLP 26 will replace the question mark.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. PS-JM does not belong to the group.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? LIF 13, DAX 24, VSP 46, NKH 90, FCZ 178, ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. XUR 354 will replace the question mark.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: This question is based on the five, three-digit numbers given below. (Left) 267 513 671 895 729 (Right) (Example: 697 - First digit = 6, second digit = 9 and third digit = 7) (NOTE: All operations to be done from left to right.) If the digits of each number are added and their respective resultants are arranged in the ascending order, what is the number placed 4th from the right end?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. The number placed 4th from the right end is 18.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: RUTW is related to TWYV in a certain way based on the English alphabetical order. In the same way, MPOR is related to ORQT. To which of the given options is VYXA related, following the same logic?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. VYXA is related to XZYC.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Shyam starts from Point A and drives 12 km towards east. He then takes a right turn, drives 7 km, turns right and drives 5 km. He then takes a left turn and drives 9 km. He takes a final right turn, drives 7 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? (All turns are 90 degrees turns only unless specified.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Shyam should drive 19 km towards north.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Seven boxes A, B, C, D, E, F and G are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only two boxes are kept between G and A. Only F is kept above B. No box is kept below A. E is kept at some place below C but at some place above D. How many boxes are kept between D and F?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Three boxes are kept between D and F.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Select the pair that follows the same pattern as that followed by the two pairs given below. Both pairs follow the same pattern. ACF:CEH PET:RGV
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. XVN:ZXP follows the same pattern.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning

questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A. SORT-TSOR-ORTS follows the same pattern.

Statements: Some birds are animals. All animals are tables. Conclusions: (I): Some birds are tables. (II): All tables are animals.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Only conclusion (I) follows.

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 36 51 45 64 54 77 63 90 72 ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. The next number in the series is 103.

He takes a final right turn, drives 5 km and stops at Point P. How far (shortest distance) and towards which direction should he drive in order to reach Point A again? questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. Babar should drive 18 km towards East.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Based on the English alphabetical order, three of the following four letter-cluster pairs are alike in a certain way and thus form a group. Which letter-cluster pair DOES NOT belong to that group? (Note: The odd one out is not based on the number of consonants/vowels or their position in the letter-cluster.)
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. PL-MI does not belong to the group.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 84 112 140 168 196 ?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. The next number in the series is 212.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: Seven boxes F, G, H, I, K, L and O are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. O is kept second from the bottom. Only three boxes are kept between O and G. L is kept at one of the positions below O. Only I is kept between G and H. F is kept at one of the positions above G. How many boxes are kept between L and F?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. Four boxes are kept between L and F.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: J, K, L, M, N, O and S are sitting around a circular table, facing the centre of the table. Only three people sit between O and J when counted from the left of O. Only three people sit between K and M when counted from the right of M. S sits to the immediate right of K. N is an immediate neighbour of M as well as J. Who sits second to the left of L?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C. J sits second to the left of L.

questions.subject: General Intelligence and Reasoning
questions.question: In the following number-pairs, the second number is obtained by applying certain mathematical operation(s) to the first number. Select the number-pair in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following pairs. (NOTE: Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into their constituent digits. E.g. 13 - Operations on 13 such as adding to/subtracting from/multiplying with 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 227,112 240,125
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option B. 254,129 follows the same pattern.
